sourceMovie.title: Moana
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Moana (2016) stuck because it commits to its adventure premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. John Musker's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The adventure and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Moana clicked for you, Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Moana?
faq.answer: Try Ice Age: Dawn of the Dinosaurs, Ice Age: Continental Drift, Hercules—each shares DNA with Moana while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Moana?
faq.answer: Moana (2016) is best described as Adventure, Comedy, Family, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Moana part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Moana Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
